statusDetail: Fully agentic audit of the seeded-issues eval fixture (eval/fixtures/seeded-issues/site) under the expanded 15-principle set (docs/principles-analysis.md, adopted 2026-06-13). The model gathered multi-modal evidence with the repo's evidence primitives (DOM+source, computed styles, screenshots, a transition video, layout metrics + a CLS/long-task observer, a heap-snapshot summary, and ad-hoc evaluate probes), then chose to run Lighthouse and to inject axe-core (via the evaluate primitive, fetched from a CDN at inspection time) to corroborate the Lighthouse-dimension principles. It judged all fifteen principles: nine were judged for real (six modern-UX + three Lighthouse dimensions, surfacing the nine seeded findings), and the contextual framework-derived principles were judged not-applicable / opted-out with rationale (see principleOutcomes) rather than penalised. No deterministic check runner was involved. This fixture is the eval ground truth; the genuinely-fixed live playground/ reports zero of these findings.

page.notes: Seeded-issues eval fixture: a hash-routed SPA where six scenarios mount scoped CSS per route. lang=en and a meta viewport are present. Audited in default (issue) mode; the ?mode=fixed run is the CSS-scenario false-positive guard. This fixture is frozen ground truth, not the live product.

evidenceUsed: dom+source (DOM, computed styles, page HTML/CSS, local fixture source)
screenshot (clipped, under emulated prefers-color-scheme: dark and a 360x800 viewport)
evaluate-probe (ad-hoc focus/animation/container probes written on the spot)
axe-core (injected from CDN via the evaluate primitive and run in-page)

config.path: (none for the frozen fixture; the repo web-uplift.json targets the live playground, not this fixture)

artifacts.caption: .ndm-card stays a white surface under an emulated dark preference
the marquee card still sliding under reduced-motion
.fl-hero clipped off-screen at a narrow mobile viewport
devtools-loadable performance trace over the load
compact trace summary (FCP/LCP, long tasks, total blocking time)
HAR 1.2 of the network over the load; the favicon 404 entry backs F-009
heap-snapshot summary by type/constructor (no leak found this run)

principleOutcomes.reason: Bare localhost static host (npx serve): no real transport, headers, cookies or auth surface to assess. The missing CSP and the favicon 404 are serving-setup artefacts shared identically by the genuinely-fixed playground, so they are not seeded fixture findings (the favicon 404 is captured under follow-best-practices as F-009).
A deliberately client-rendered CSS-scenario demo; the offline/installable aspect is out of scope and the no-JS blank shell is a shared demo-harness property, not a seeded issue.
Single-locale English demo with lang=en and no locale-sensitive data (dates, numbers, currencies, calendars).
The Save/Cancel/Delete buttons are demo-only with no real action, no forms to validate, and no dark patterns, upsells or consent walls.
A tiny hand-authored demo (a few KB of HTML/CSS/JS); page weight is already minimal, judged proportionally.
Static UX demo with no agent-facing surface; not exposing WebMCP tools or on-device inference (emerging, judged as opportunity not obligation).

paths.description: Account-settings card under an emulated dark preference
Sliding marquee card under an emulated reduced-motion preference
Fixed 1200px hero/column at an emulated narrow mobile viewport
Buttons with outline removed; keyboard focus visibility and contrast
Late-injected banner with no reserved space; CLS observation
A reused media card in a wide vs a 240px-narrow container
conditions: component reused at two container widths

paths.description: Document-level accessibility / best-practices / SEO signal via Lighthouse + axe

findings.summary: Surface ignores prefers-color-scheme: dark and stays a light card.
findings.evidence: Under emulated prefers-color-scheme: dark, a computed-style probe and a clipped screenshot of .ndm-card show background rgb(255, 255, 255) (a glaring white surface). The card hard-codes background: #ffffff; color: #111111 with no light-dark() and no per-element color-scheme. Re-tinting fails purely because the adaptation is missing.
findings.suggestedFix: Declare color-scheme: light dark on the card (or root) and use light-dark(#ffffff, #1e1e1e) for surfaces and light-dark(#111111, #eeeeee) for text so the card follows the user preference. See Modern Web Guidance id dark-mode.

findings.summary: Animation keeps running under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.
findings.evidence: Under emulated prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, an evaluate probe of .mv-card.getAnimations() returned 1 running animation (animation-name mv-slide); a 2.5s transition video recorded under the reduce preference shows the card still sliding. The animation is applied unconditionally rather than gated behind a no-preference media query.
findings.suggestedFix: Gate the marquee animation behind @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) so it only runs when the user has not requested reduced motion.

findings.summary: Fixed 1200px layout overflows a narrow mobile viewport.
findings.evidence: At an emulated 360x800 viewport the layout primitive reported horizontal overflow of 1158px (document scrollWidth far exceeds visualViewport.width 360); a screenshot at 360px shows the blue .fl-hero (computed width ~1264px) clipped off-screen. The hero and body hard-code width: 1200px.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace the fixed width: 1200px with width: 100%; max-width: 1200px; box-sizing: border-box so the layout adapts down to small screens.

findings.summary: Focus outline removed with no :focus-visible replacement.
findings.evidence: An evaluate probe that focuses .pf-btn and reads the computed outline returned outline-style: none; CSS inspection found .pf-btn { outline: none } and no :focus-visible rule restoring an outline. Keyboard users get no visible focus indicator.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove the blanket outline: none and add .pf-btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #1a73e8; outline-offset: 2px } so keyboard focus is clearly indicated without affecting pointer clicks.

findings.summary: Cumulative layout shift from a late banner with no reserved space.
findings.evidence: The layout primitive's layout-shift observer recorded CLS 0.0118 across 2 shifts as the banner injected at ~600ms pushed following content down; the .ls-slot reserves no height. CLS is small here because the page is tall, but the mechanism (unreserved late content) is the classic CLS anti-pattern and scales with banner size.
findings.suggestedFix: Reserve the banner's space up front with min-height (or aspect-ratio) on .ls-slot so the late content does not shift surrounding layout.

findings.summary: Reused component does not adapt to its container (no container queries).
findings.evidence: An evaluate probe found the same .cq-card stays flex-direction: row inside the 240px-wide .cq-narrow container (cramping the 80px thumbnail against the text), and no @container rule or container-type is declared. The component responds only to the viewport, not its container.
findings.suggestedFix: Add container-type: inline-size to the wrapper and a @container (max-width: 320px) rule that stacks the card (flex-direction: column; thumb width: 100%). See guidance id size-aware-styling.

findings.evidence: Lighthouse (accessibility 91) flagged color-contrast: score 0 on the three .pf-btn buttons; axe-core, injected from CDN via the evaluate primitive and run in-page, independently reported one serious color-contrast violation across 3 nodes. The grey #f2f2f2 button background with the default text does not meet the contrast threshold. This was not one of the playground's six explicitly seeded CSS scenarios; the agentic audit surfaced it from the Lighthouse-dimension principles.
findings.suggestedFix: Raise the button text/background contrast to at least 4.5:1 for body text (e.g. darken the label or the border, or use a higher-contrast surface). Verify with axe / Lighthouse after the change.

findings.evidence: Lighthouse SEO (90) reported meta-description: score 0 (missing); the recon DOM dump confirms <head> has a <title> but no <meta name="description">. A descriptive title is present, so this is a single missing tag.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a concise, descriptive <meta name="description"> to the document head so search and share previews have summary text.

findings.summary: A resource 404s in the console on load.
findings.evidence: Lighthouse best-practices (96) reported errors-in-console: score 0, with one entry: 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)' (a missing resource, the favicon, under the bare serve setup with no <link rel=icon>). Adding a favicon eliminates it, so it is a real, fixable defect rather than a pure environment artefact.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a favicon (e.g. an inline data: SVG <link rel="icon">) or otherwise resolve the missing resource so the page loads without console errors.

taskList.title: Adopt color-scheme + light-dark() so the card follows the dark preference
Gate the marquee animation behind prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference
Make the fixed 1200px layout fluid (width 100% + max-width)
Restore a visible keyboard focus indicator with :focus-visible
Raise button colour contrast to meet WCAG AA
Reserve space for the late banner to remove the layout shift
Use a container query so the reused card adapts to its container
Add a favicon to eliminate the console 404 on load

eval.subject: eval/fixtures/seeded-issues/site (the frozen ground-truth fixture)
eval.joinKey: findingId / scenario id; all nine expected findings (six CSS scenarios + three page-level Lighthouse-dimension findings) are counted
eval.note: All nine ground-truth findings were surfaced against the fixture (100% recall, 0 false positives) under the expanded 15-principle set. The six net-new / changed principles added no spurious fixture findings: be-inclusive (was be-accessible) still owns the contrast finding F-007; follow-best-practices (narrowed) still owns the console-404 F-009; and the contextual framework-derived principles (be-private-and-secure, be-resilient, be-internationalised, be-sustainable, be-agent-ready) were correctly judged not-applicable / opted-out rather than penalised. The genuinely-fixed live playground/ (audited separately) surfaced ZERO of the nine: dark surface re-tints to rgb(30,30,30), 0 running animations under reduced-motion, 0px horizontal overflow at 360px (the shell grid collapses below 640px), solid focus outline, flex-direction column in the narrow container, CLS 0.0041, a meta description present, axe color-contrast violations 0, and Lighthouse 100/100/100/100 (a11y/best-practices/seo/perf); its contextual-principle outcomes match the fixture's (same not-applicable/opted-out rationale). The fixture's ?mode=fixed run is the CSS-scenario false-positive guard.
